description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural, unfinished appearance and visible wood grain. The handle is ergonomically shaped, widening at the base for a secure grip and narrowing toward the head. The axe head is made of dark, weathered metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade that tapers to a sharp edge. The blade is securely mounted to the handle, with the poll (back of the head) squared off and the cheeks of the blade showing some wear and patina. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding. This tool is typical of those found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance sheds.
description: A sturdy hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, weathered metal blade, designed for practical use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the curved wooden handle and the wide, sharp metal blade.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the blade's wedge shape from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the axe head and the handle's thickness.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural finish, ergonomic grip, oval cross-section, and flared base for secure handling.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a broad cutting edge, squared poll, and no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle and secured by friction.
